  /v1/marketplace/servers/{registryId}/install:
    parameters:
      - name: registryId
        in: path
        required: true
        schema: { type: string }
        description: Registry identifier (e.g. io.github.user/server)
    post:
      tags: [Marketplace]
      summary: Install an MCP server from the marketplace
      operationId: installMarketplaceServer
      responses:
        "201":
          description: Server installed
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/InstallResult"
        "404":
          description: Server not found in registry

components:
  securitySchemes:
    bearerAuth:
      type: http
      scheme: bearer
      bearerFormat: JWT

  parameters:
    IntentId:
      name: intent_id
      in: path
      required: true
      schema: { type: string }
    ApprovalId:
      name: approval_id
      in: path
      required: true
      schema: { type: string }
    PolicyId:
      name: policy_id
      in: path
      required: true
      schema: { type: string }
    ConnectorId:
      name: connector_id
      in: path
      required: true
      schema: { type: string }
    GrantId:
      name: grant_id
      in: path
      required: true
      schema: { type: string }
    ExecutionId:
      name: execution_id
      in: path
      required: true
      schema: { type: string }
    PageSize:
      name: page_size
      in: query
      schema:
        type: integer
        minimum: 1
        maximum: 200
        default: 50
    PageToken:
      name: page_token
      in: query
      schema:
        type: string

  responses:
    BadRequest:
      description: Invalid request
      content:
        application/json:
          schema:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/Error"
    Unauthorized:
      description: Missing or invalid authentication
      content:
        application/json:
          schema:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/Error"
    Forbidden:
      description: Insufficient permissions
      content:
        application/json:
          schema:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/Error"
    NotFound:
      description: Resource not found
      content:
        application/json:
          schema:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/Error"
    Conflict:
      description: Conflict with current resource state
      content:
        application/json:
          schema:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/Error"

  schemas:
    HealthResponse:
      type: object
      required: [status, service, version, timestamp]
      properties:
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [ok, degraded, down]
        service:
          type: string
        version:
          type: string
        timestamp:
          type: string
          format: date-time

    Error:
      type: object
      required: [error]
      properties:
        error:
          type: object
          required: [code, message]
          properties:
            code: { type: string }
            message: { type: string }
            details:
              type: array
              items:
                type: object
                additionalProperties: true
            request_id: { type: string }

    Pagination:
      type: object
      properties:
        next_page_token:
          type: string
          nullable: true

    IntentStatus:
      type: string
      enum: [pending_policy, pending_approval, approved, denied, executing, succeeded, failed, cancelled, expired]

    ApprovalStatus:
      type: string
      enum: [pending, approved, denied, modified, delegated, expired, cancelled]

    PolicyStatus:
      type: string
      enum: [draft, active, archived]

    ConnectorType:
      type: string
      enum: [payments, source_control, cloud, deployment, email, calendar, ticketing, crm, erp, secret_manager, internal_api, custom]

    RiskLevel:
      type: string
      enum: [low, medium, high, critical]

    DecisionDisposition:
      type: string
      enum: [allow, allow_modified, require_approval, deny, require_more_evidence]

    ActorRef:
      type: object
      required: [id, type]
      properties:
        id: { type: string }
        type:
          type: string
          enum: [agent, human, service, connector_runtime]
        display_name: { type: string }

    ActionTarget:
      type: object
      required: [kind]
      properties:
        kind:
          type: string
          enum: [vendor, repository, environment, api, secret, calendar, email_recipient, ticket, cloud_resource, custom]
        id: { type: string }
        display_name: { type: string }

    Money:
      type: object
      required: [amount, currency]
      properties:
        amount:
          type: integer
          description: Amount in minor units
          minimum: 0
        currency:
          type: string
          minLength: 3
          maxLength: 3

    Recipient:
      type: object
      required: [email]
      properties:
        name: { type: string }
        email:
          type: string
          format: email

    AttachmentRef:
      type: object
      properties:
        name: { type: string }
        mime_type: { type: string }
        url: { type: string, format: uri }
        storage_ref: { type: string }

    CalendarAttendee:
      type: object
      required: [email]
      properties:
        name: { type: string }
        email:
          type: string
          format: email
        optional:
          type: boolean
          default: false

    LineItem:
      type: object
      required: [description, quantity, unit_amount]
      properties:
        description:
          type: string
        quantity:
          type: integer
          minimum: 1
        unit_amount:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/Money"
        sku:
          type: string

    GitAction:
      type: object
      properties:
        provider:
          type: string
          enum: [github, gitlab, bitbucket, custom]
        repository:
          type: string
        branch:
          type: string
        base_branch:
          type: string
        commit_shas:
          type: array
          items:
            type: string
        files_changed:
          type: array
          items:
            type: string
        pr_title:
          type: string
        pr_body:
          type: string
        labels:
          type: array
          items:
            type: string
        reviewers:
          type: array
          items:
            type: string
        checks_required:
          type: array
          items:
            type: string

    DeployAction:
      type: object
      properties:
        provider:
          type: string
          enum: [kubernetes, vercel, netlify, aws, gcp, azure, flyio, custom]
        service:
          type: string
        environment:
          type: string
        artifact_ref:
          type: string
        image_ref:
          type: string
        cluster:
          type: string
        namespace:
          type: string
        strategy:
          type: string
          enum: [rolling, blue_green, canary, replace, custom]
        rollback_supported:
          type: boolean
        change_ticket_id:
          type: string
        maintenance_window_id:
          type: string

    CloudAction:
      type: object
      properties:
        provider:
          type: string
          enum: [aws, gcp, azure, cloudflare, custom]
        account_id:
          type: string
        region:
          type: string
        resource_type:
          type: string
        resource_id:
          type: string
        operation:
          type: string
        estimated_cost:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/Money"
        tags:
          type: object
          additionalProperties:
            type: string

    EmailAction:
      type: object
      properties:
        from:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/Recipient"
        to:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/Recipient"
        cc:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/Recipient"
        bcc:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/Recipient"
        subject:
          type: string
        body_text:
          type: string
        body_html:
          type: string
        attachments:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/AttachmentRef"
        thread_ref:
          type: string
        send_mode:
          type: string
          enum: [send_now, draft_only]

    CalendarAction:
      type: object
      properties:
        provider:
          type: string
          enum: [google_calendar, outlook, custom]
        title:
          type: string
        description:
          type: string
        attendees:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/CalendarAttendee"
        start_time:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        end_time:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        timezone:
          type: string
        location:
          type: string
        conference_type:
          type: string
          enum: [none, google_meet, zoom, teams, custom]
        calendar_id:
          type: string
        visibility:
          type: string
          enum: [default, public, private]

    PaymentBeneficiary:
      type: object
      properties:
        name: { type: string }
        email:
          type: string
          format: email
        department: { type: string }

    PaymentAction:
      type: object
      properties:
        vendor_name:
          type: string
        vendor_id:
          type: string
        amount:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/Money"
        merchant_category:
          type: string
        budget_code:
          type: string
        funding_source_id:
          type: string
        payment_instrument_preference:
          type: string
          enum: [virtual_card, ach, wallet, network_proxy, unspecified]
        beneficiary:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/PaymentBeneficiary"
        line_items:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/LineItem"
        renewal:
          type: boolean
        contract_term:
          type: string
        recurring_interval:
          type: string
          enum: [none, monthly, quarterly, annual]
        merchant_reference:
          type: string

    ProcurementAction:
      type: object
      properties:
        request_type:
          type: string
          enum: [software, travel, equipment, contractor, services, other]
        vendor_name:
          type: string
        amount_estimate:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/Money"
        justification:
          type: string
        requestor:
          type: string
        cost_center:
          type: string
        legal_review_required:
          type: boolean
        security_review_required:
          type: boolean
        line_items:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/LineItem"

    DomainAction:
      type: object
      description: |
        Carries action-type-specific fields. Exactly one field must be populated,
        corresponding to the prefix of ActionIntent.type:
          - git.* → git
          - deploy.* → deploy
          - cloud.* → cloud
          - email.* → email
          - calendar.* → calendar
          - payment.* → payment
          - procurement.* → procurement
        The server rejects requests where the populated domain field does not
        match the action type prefix.
      minProperties: 1
      maxProperties: 1
      properties:
        git:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/GitAction"
        deploy:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/DeployAction"
        cloud:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/CloudAction"
        email:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/EmailAction"
        calendar:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/CalendarAction"
        payment:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/PaymentAction"
        procurement:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/ProcurementAction"

    ActionIntent:
      type: object
      required: [type, summary]
      properties:
        type:
          type: string
          examples:
            - git.pull_request.create
            - deploy.release
            - cloud.resource.mutate
            - email.send
            - calendar.event.create
            - payment.charge
            - procurement.request.submit
        summary:
          type: string
        justification:
          type: string
        target:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/ActionTarget"
        domain:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/DomainAction"
        metadata:
          type: object
          additionalProperties: true

    IntentContext:
      type: object
      properties:
        environment:
          type: string
          examples: [development, staging, production]
        source_platform:
          type: string
          examples: [openclaw, claude_code, codex, openhands, goose, cline, continue, langgraph, custom]
        source_session_id:
          type: string
        source_trace_id:
          type: string
        ip_address:
          type: string
        risk_hints:
          type: array
          items:
            type: string
        user_present:
          type: boolean
        temporary_grant_id:
          type: string

    CreateIntentRequest:
      type: object
      required: [idempotency_key, agent_id, workspace_id, action]
      properties:
        idempotency_key:
          type: string
        agent_id:
          type: string
        workspace_id:
          type: string
        action:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/ActionIntent"
        context:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/IntentContext"
        callback_url:
          type: string
          format: uri
          description: |
            Optional webhook URL. The control plane will POST status updates
            to this URL as the intent progresses through the lifecycle
            (policy evaluated, approval requested, approved/denied, execution
            started, execution completed). This avoids the need for agents
            to poll the GET /v1/intents/{id} endpoint.

    EvidenceItem:
      type: object
      required: [type, value]
      properties:
        type:
          type: string
          enum: [text, link, diff_summary, screenshot_ref, receipt_ref, test_result, change_ticket, artifact_ref, custom]
        label:
          type: string
        value:
          type: string

    AppendEvidenceRequest:
      type: object
      required: [evidence]
      properties:
        evidence:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/EvidenceItem"

    PolicyMatch:
      type: object
      properties:
        policy_id: { type: string }
        policy_version: { type: integer }
        rule_id: { type: string }
        explanation: { type: string }

    Decision:
      type: object
      required: [disposition, risk_level]
      properties:
        disposition:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/DecisionDisposition"
        risk_level:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/RiskLevel"
        matched_policies:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/PolicyMatch"
        requires_approval:
          type: boolean
        approval_id:
          type: string
          nullable: true
        execution_grant_id:
          type: string
          nullable: true
        denial_reason:
          type: string
          nullable: true

    IntentEnvelope:
      type: object
      required: [intent_id, status, workspace_id, agent, action, decision, created_at, updated_at]
      properties:
        intent_id:
          type: string
        workspace_id:
          type: string
        status:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/IntentStatus"
        agent:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/ActorRef"
        action:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/ActionIntent"
        context:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/IntentContext"
        decision:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/Decision"
        evidence:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/EvidenceItem"
        created_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        updated_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time

    IntentListResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        items:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/IntentEnvelope"
        pagination:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/Pagination"

    ApprovalActor:
      type: object
      required: [principal_id]
      properties:
        principal_id:
          type: string
        display_name:
          type: string
        role:
          type: string
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [pending, approved, denied, delegated]

    Approval:
      type: object
      required: [approval_id, intent_id, status, requested_at, approvers]
      properties:
        approval_id:
          type: string
        intent_id:
          type: string
        workspace_id:
          type: string
        status:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/ApprovalStatus"
        rationale:
          type: string
        approvers:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/ApprovalActor"
        expires_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time
          nullable: true
        requested_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        resolved_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time
          nullable: true
        editable_bounds:
          type: object
          additionalProperties: true

    ApprovalListResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        items:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/Approval"
        pagination:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/Pagination"

    ApproveRequest:
      type: object
      properties:
        comment:
          type: string
        approve_once:
          type: boolean
          default: false
        step_up_auth_assertion:
          type: string

    DenyRequest:
      type: object
      required: [reason]
      properties:
        reason:
          type: string
        comment:
          type: string

    ModifyApprovalRequest:
      type: object
      required: [modifications]
      properties:
        comment:
          type: string
        modifications:
          type: object
          additionalProperties: true

    ApprovalActionResponse:
      type: object
      required: [approval_id, status]
      properties:
        approval_id:
          type: string
        status:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/ApprovalStatus"
        intent_status:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/IntentStatus"
        execution_grant_id:
          type: string
          nullable: true

    PolicyCondition:
      type: object
      properties:
        field:
          type: string
        operator:
          type: string
          enum: [eq, neq, in, not_in, gt, gte, lt, lte, contains, matches]
        value:
          oneOf:
            - type: string
            - type: number
            - type: integer
            - type: boolean
            - type: array
              items: {}

    ApprovalRequirement:
      type: object
      properties:
        mode:
          type: string
          enum: [single, any_of_group, all_of_group, quorum]
        approver_group_id:
          type: string
        quorum_count:
          type: integer
          minimum: 1

    PolicyRule:
      type: object
      required: [rule_id, effect]
      properties:
        rule_id:
          type: string
        description:
          type: string
        conditions:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/PolicyCondition"
        effect:
          type: string
          enum: [allow, deny, require_approval, allow_with_modification]
        approval_requirement:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/ApprovalRequirement"
        modifications_template:
          type: object
          additionalProperties: true
        priority:
          type: integer
          default: 100

    Policy:
      type: object
      required: [policy_id, name, workspace_id, status, version, rules]
      properties:
        policy_id:
          type: string
        workspace_id:
          type: string
        name:
          type: string
        description:
          type: string
        environment:
          type: string
        status:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/PolicyStatus"
        version:
          type: integer
        rules:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/PolicyRule"
        created_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        updated_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time

    CreatePolicyRequest:
      type: object
      required: [workspace_id, name, rules]
      properties:
        workspace_id:
          type: string
        name:
          type: string
        description:
          type: string
        environment:
          type: string
        status:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/PolicyStatus"
        rules:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/PolicyRule"

    UpdatePolicyRequest:
      type: object
      properties:
        name:
          type: string
        description:
          type: string
        status:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/PolicyStatus"
        rules:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/PolicyRule"

    PolicyListResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        items:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/Policy"
        pagination:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/Pagination"

    PolicySimulationRequest:
      type: object
      required: [workspace_id, action]
      properties:
        workspace_id:
          type: string
        action:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/ActionIntent"
        context:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/IntentContext"

    PolicySimulationResponse:
      type: object
      required: [decision]
      properties:
        decision:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/Decision"

    ConnectorAuth:
      type: object
      required: [method]
      properties:
        method:
          type: string
          enum: [oauth2, api_key_ref, service_account_ref, webhook_secret_ref, custom]
        reference_id:
          type: string
        scopes:
          type: array
          items:
            type: string

    Connector:
      type: object
      required: [connector_id, workspace_id, type, name, status]
      properties:
        connector_id:
          type: string
        workspace_id:
          type: string
        type:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/ConnectorType"
        name:
          type: string
        provider:
          type: string
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [active, disabled, error]
        environment:
          type: string
        auth:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/ConnectorAuth"
        policy_ids:
          type: array
          items:
            type: string
        metadata:
          type: object
          additionalProperties: true

    CreateConnectorRequest:
      type: object
      required: [workspace_id, type, name, auth]
      properties:
        workspace_id:
          type: string
        type:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/ConnectorType"
        name:
          type: string
        provider:
          type: string
        environment:
          type: string
        auth:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/ConnectorAuth"
        metadata:
          type: object
          additionalProperties: true

    UpdateConnectorRequest:
      type: object
      properties:
        name:
          type: string
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [active, disabled]
        auth:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/ConnectorAuth"
        metadata:
          type: object
          additionalProperties: true

    ConnectorListResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        items:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/Connector"
        pagination:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/Pagination"

    ExecutionGrant:
      type: object
      required: [grant_id, intent_id, status, expires_at]
      properties:
        grant_id:
          type: string
        intent_id:
          type: string
        connector_id:
          type: string
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [active, consumed, revoked, expired]
        bounded_parameters:
          type: object
          additionalProperties: true
        expires_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time

    ExecutionRunRequest:
      type: object
      required: [grant_id]
      properties:
        grant_id:
          type: string
        override_parameters:
          type: object
          additionalProperties: true

    ExecutionRunResponse:
      type: object
      required: [execution_id, status]
      properties:
        execution_id:
          type: string
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [accepted, running]
        accepted_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time

    Execution:
      type: object
      required: [execution_id, intent_id, status, started_at]
      properties:
        execution_id:
          type: string
        intent_id:
          type: string
        connector_id:
          type: string
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [accepted, running, succeeded, failed, cancelled]
        output:
          type: object
          additionalProperties: true
        receipt_ref:
          type: string
          nullable: true
        started_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        finished_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time
          nullable: true

    ExecutionCallbackRequest:
      type: object
      required: [status]
      properties:
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [running, succeeded, failed, cancelled]
        output:
          type: object
          additionalProperties: true
        receipt_ref:
          type: string
        error_message:
          type: string

    FundingSource:
      type: object
      required: [funding_source_id, workspace_id, type, name, status]
      properties:
        funding_source_id:
          type: string
        workspace_id:
          type: string
        type:
          type: string
          enum: [virtual_card_pool, budget, fiat_account_ref, lightning_wallet_ref, btc_wallet_ref, stored_balance]
        name:
          type: string
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [active, disabled]
        currency:
          type: string
        spending_controls:
          type: object
          properties:
            max_single_transaction:
              $ref: "#/components/schemas/Money"
            allowed_merchants:
              type: array
              items:
                type: string
            allowed_categories:
              type: array
              items:
                type: string
        metadata:
          type: object
          additionalProperties: true

    CreateFundingSourceRequest:
      type: object
      required: [workspace_id, type, name]
      properties:
        workspace_id:
          type: string
        type:
          type: string
          enum: [virtual_card_pool, budget, fiat_account_ref, lightning_wallet_ref, btc_wallet_ref, stored_balance]
        name:
          type: string
        currency:
          type: string
        spending_controls:
          type: object
          additionalProperties: true
        metadata:
          type: object
          additionalProperties: true

    FundingSourceListResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        items:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/FundingSource"
        pagination:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/Pagination"

    CredentialReference:
      type: object
      required: [credential_id, workspace_id, type, name, vault_path]
      properties:
        credential_id:
          type: string
        workspace_id:
          type: string
        type:
          type: string
          enum: [api_key, oauth_refresh_token, service_account, webhook_secret, custom]
        name:
          type: string
        vault_path:
          type: string
        environment:
          type: string
        metadata:
          type: object
          additionalProperties: true

    CreateCredentialRequest:
      type: object
      required: [workspace_id, type, name, vault_path]
      properties:
        workspace_id:
          type: string
        type:
          type: string
          enum: [api_key, oauth_refresh_token, service_account, webhook_secret, custom]
        name:
          type: string
        vault_path:
          type: string
        environment:
          type: string
        metadata:
          type: object
          additionalProperties: true

    CredentialListResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        items:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/CredentialReference"
        pagination:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/Pagination"

    TraceEvent:
      type: object
      required: [event_id, event_type, actor, timestamp]
      properties:
        event_id:
          type: string
        event_type:
          type: string
        actor:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/ActorRef"
        payload:
          type: object
          additionalProperties: true
        timestamp:
          type: string
          format: date-time

    Trace:
      type: object
      required: [trace_id, intent_id, events]
      properties:
        trace_id:
          type: string
        intent_id:
          type: string
        workspace_id:
          type: string
        events:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/TraceEvent"

    TraceListResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        items:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/Trace"
        pagination:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/Pagination"

    AnalyticsSummary:
      type: object
      properties:
        totals:
          type: object
          properties:
            intents: { type: integer }
            approvals_requested: { type: integer }
            approvals_granted: { type: integer }
            denials: { type: integer }
            executions_succeeded: { type: integer }
            executions_failed: { type: integer }
        spend:
          type: object
          properties:
            total_minor_units: { type: integer }
            currency: { type: string }
        approval_latency_ms:
          type: object
          properties:
            p50: { type: integer }
            p95: { type: integer }
        by_risk_level:
          type: object
          additionalProperties:
            type: integer

    MCPServerConfig:
      type: object
      required: [name, command]
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
          readOnly: true
        name:
          type: string
          description: Unique identifier for this server
        description:
          type: string
          description: Human-readable description of this server
        command:
          type: array
          items:
            type: string
          description: Command to execute
        env:
          type: object
          additionalProperties:
            type: string
          description: Environment variables (vault:// prefix for vault resolution)
        mode:
          type: string
          enum: [local, remote]
          default: local
          description: Execution mode (local subprocess or remote HTTP)
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [stopped, running, error]
          readOnly: true
          description: Current server status (set by the system)
        registry_id:
          type: string
          description: MCP Registry identifier if installed from marketplace
        policy_mapping:
          type: object
          properties:
            tool_prefix:
              type: string
              description: Maps tool calls to action type namespace
        created_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time
          readOnly: true
        updated_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time
          readOnly: true

    SetCredentialRequest:
      type: object
      required: [env_var_name, secret_value]
      properties:
        env_var_name:
          type: string
          description: Environment variable name
        secret_value:
          type: string
          description: Secret value to store in the vault

    SetCredentialResponse:
      type: object
      required: [env_var_name, stored]
      properties:
        env_var_name:
          type: string
        stored:
          type: boolean

    MarketplaceServer:
      type: object
      required: [registry_id, name]
      properties:
        registry_id:
          type: string
          description: Unique registry identifier (reverse DNS)
        name:
          type: string
        description:
          type: string
        version:
          type: string
        installed:
          type: boolean
          description: Whether this server is already installed locally
        required_env_vars:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/RequiredEnvVar"

    RequiredEnvVar:
      type: object
      required: [name]
      properties:
        name:
          type: string
        description:
          type: string
        required:
          type: boolean

    InstallResult:
      type: object
      required: [server]
      properties:
        server:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/MCPServerConfig"
        required_credentials:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/RequiredEnvVar"

content.text:
Pushed. Here's what changed:

**Backend:**
- `MarketplaceServer` schema now has `versions: MarketplaceServerVersion[]` instead of a single `version` string. Each version entry carries its own `required_env_vars`.
- The handler groups registry entries by name, collects all versions, and reverses them so most recent is first. Response items maintain stable ordering.

**UI:**
- Each card shows the server name, description, and latest version number with a count (e.g. "v2.1.0 (6 versions)")
- Single-version servers install directly on click
- Multi-version servers expand a dropdown with all versions (most recent pre-selected) and a confirm Install button

message.content: Add a pre-fetch and timed job in the server service to prefetch the list of servers from the registry. The user clicking the UI for the first time takes too long to respond for the full list. And run this job to refresh the reistry list every 15 minutes. Then we don't need a timed cache expiry; it'll just refresh every 15 minutes. Make the 15 minutes a configurable property that can be overridden by folks running the server service. And document it; 15 minutes is a default.

1→// Package main is the entry point for the Aileron control plane server.
     2→package main
     3→
     4→import (
     5→	"context"
     6→	"log/slog"
     7→	"net/http"
     8→	"os"
     9→	"os/signal"
    10→	"syscall"
    11→	"time"
    12→
    13→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/app"
    14→)
    15→
    16→func main() {
    17→	log := slog.New(slog.NewJSONHandler(os.Stdout, nil))
    18→
    19→	if err := run(log); err != nil {
    20→		log.Error("server exited with error", "error", err)
    21→		os.Exit(1)
    22→	}
    23→}
    24→
    25→func run(log *slog.Logger) error {
    26→	addr := os.Getenv("AILERON_ADDR")
    27→	if addr == "" {
    28→		addr = ":8080"
    29→	}
    30→
    31→	handler, err := app.NewHandler(log)
    32→	if err != nil {
    33→		return err
    34→	}
    35→
    36→	srv := &http.Server{
    37→		Addr:         addr,
    38→		Handler:      handler,
    39→		ReadTimeout:  10 * time.Second,
    40→		WriteTimeout: 30 * time.Second,
    41→		IdleTimeout:  120 * time.Second,
    42→	}
    43→
    44→	// Graceful shutdown on SIGINT / SIGTERM.
    45→	ctx, stop := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
    46→	defer stop()
    47→
    48→	go func() {
    49→		log.Info("server listening", "addr", addr)
    50→		if err := srv.ListenAndServe(); err != nil && err != http.ErrServerClosed {
    51→			log.Error("listen error", "error", err)
    52→		}
    53→	}()
    54→
    55→	<-ctx.Done()
    56→	log.Info("shutting down")
    57→
    58→	shutdownCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)
    59→	defer cancel()
    60→
    61→	return srv.Shutdown(shutdownCtx)
    62→}
    63→

1→// Package main implements the Aileron MCP gateway server.
     2→//
     3→// The gateway is the single MCP server that an agent host (Claude Code, etc.)
     4→// connects to. It aggregates tools from downstream MCP servers, re-exposes
     5→// them under namespaced names, and intercepts every tools/call for governance.
     6→//
     7→// It communicates over stdio using JSON-RPC 2.0, per the MCP specification.
     8→//
     9→// Modes:
    10→//   - Embedded (default): runs an in-process Aileron server with in-memory stores.
    11→//   - Remote: set AILERON_API_URL to connect to an existing Aileron server.
    12→package main
    13→
    14→import (
    15→	"bufio"
    16→	"context"
    17→	"encoding/json"
    18→	"fmt"
    19→	"io"
    20→	"log/slog"
    21→	"net"
    22→	"net/http"
    23→	"os"
    24→	"os/signal"
    25→	"syscall"
    26→	"time"
    27→
    28→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/app"
    29→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/approval"
    30→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/config"
    31→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/policy"
    32→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/store/mem"
    33→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/vault"
    34→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/version"
    35→	"github.com/google/uuid"
    36→)
    37→
    38→// --- JSON-RPC types ---
    39→
    40→type jsonrpcRequest struct {
    41→	JSONRPC string          `json:"jsonrpc"`
    42→	ID      json.RawMessage `json:"id,omitempty"`
    43→	Method  string          `json:"method"`
    44→	Params  json.RawMessage `json:"params,omitempty"`
    45→}
    46→
    47→type jsonrpcResponse struct {
    48→	JSONRPC string          `json:"jsonrpc"`
    49→	ID      json.RawMessage `json:"id,omitempty"`
    50→	Result  any             `json:"result,omitempty"`
    51→	Error   *jsonrpcError   `json:"error,omitempty"`
    52→}
    53→
    54→type jsonrpcError struct {
    55→	Code    int    `json:"code"`
    56→	Message string `json:"message"`
    57→}
    58→
    59→// --- MCP types ---
    60→
    61→type toolDef struct {
    62→	Name        string `json:"name"`
    63→	Description string `json:"description"`
    64→	InputSchema schema `json:"inputSchema"`
    65→}
    66→
    67→type schema struct {
    68→	Type       string                `json:"type"`
    69→	Properties map[string]schemaProp `json:"properties,omitempty"`
    70→	Required   []string              `json:"required,omitempty"`
    71→}
    72→
    73→type schemaProp struct {
    74→	Type        string `json:"type"`
    75→	Description string `json:"description"`
    76→}
    77→
    78→type callToolParams struct {
    79→	Name      string         `json:"name"`
    80→	Arguments map[string]any `json:"arguments"`
    81→}
    82→
    83→type toolResult struct {
    84→	Content []toolContent `json:"content"`
    85→	IsError bool          `json:"isError,omitempty"`
    86→}
    87→
    88→type toolContent struct {
    89→	Type string `json:"type"`
    90→	Text string `json:"text"`
    91→}
    92→
    93→// --- Server ---
    94→
    95→type server struct {
    96→	gw  *gateway
    97→	log *slog.Logger
    98→}
    99→
   100→func main() {
   101→	log := slog.New(slog.NewJSONHandler(os.Stderr, &slog.HandlerOptions{Level: slog.LevelInfo}))
   102→	ctx := context.Background()
   103→
   104→	// Start embedded Aileron server if no remote URL is configured.
   105→	// This provides the control plane (policy engine, approval orchestrator, vault).
   106→	var v vault.Vault
   107→	apiURL := os.Getenv("AILERON_API_URL")
   108→	if apiURL == "" {
   109→		discardLog := slog.New(slog.NewJSONHandler(io.Discard, nil))
   110→		handler, err := app.NewHandler(discardLog)
   111→		if err != nil {
   112→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "aileron-mcp: failed to start embedded server: %v\n", err)
   113→			os.Exit(1)
   114→		}
   115→		listener, err := net.Listen("tcp", "127.0.0.1:0")
   116→		if err != nil {
   117→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "aileron-mcp: failed to listen: %v\n", err)
   118→			os.Exit(1)
   119→		}
   120→		go http.Serve(listener, handler)
   121→		apiURL = "http://" + listener.Addr().String()
   122→
   123→		// In embedded mode, create an in-memory vault for credential injection.
   124→		v = vault.NewMemVault()
   125→	}
   126→
   127→	// Load gateway configuration.
   128→	cfg, err := config.LoadFromEnvOrDefault()
   129→	if err != nil {
   130→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "aileron-mcp: failed to load config: %v\n", err)
   131→		os.Exit(1)
   132→	}
   133→
   134→	// Build policy engine with seeded policies.
   135→	policyStore := mem.NewPolicyStore()
   136→	if err := policy.SeedPolicies(ctx, policyStore); err != nil {
   137→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "aileron-mcp: failed to seed policies: %v\n", err)
   138→		os.Exit(1)
   139→	}
   140→	policyEngine := policy.NewRuleEngine(policyStore)
   141→
   142→	// Build approval orchestrator.
   143→	approvalStore := mem.NewApprovalStore()
   144→	idGen := func() string { return uuid.New().String() }
   145→	orchestrator := approval.NewInMemoryOrchestrator(approvalStore, idGen)
   146→
   147→	// Build in-memory audit store.
   148→	auditStore := newMemAuditStore()
   149→
   150→	// Connect to downstream MCP servers.
   151→	gw, err := newGateway(ctx, gatewayConfig{
   152→		Cfg:          cfg,
   153→		APIURL:       apiURL,
   154→		Vault:        v,
   155→		PolicyEngine: policyEngine,
   156→		Approvals:    orchestrator,
   157→		AuditStore:   auditStore,
   158→		Log:          log,
   159→	})
   160→	if err != nil {
   161→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "aileron-mcp: failed to start gateway: %v\n", err)
   162→		os.Exit(1)
   163→	}
   164→
   165→	// Handle SIGTERM and SIGINT for graceful shutdown.
   166→	sigCh := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
   167→	signal.Notify(sigCh, syscall.SIGTERM, syscall.SIGINT)
   168→	go func() {
   169→		sig := <-sigCh
   170→		log.Info("received signal, shutting down", "signal", sig.String())
   171→		shutdownCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
   172→		defer cancel()
   173→		gw.Shutdown(shutdownCtx)
   174→		os.Exit(0)
   175→	}()
   176→	defer gw.closeAll()
   177→
   178→	s := &server{gw: gw, log: log}
   179→
   180→	scanner := bufio.NewScanner(os.Stdin)
   181→	scanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 1024*1024), 1024*1024)
   182→
   183→	for scanner.Scan() {
   184→		line := scanner.Bytes()
   185→		if len(line) == 0 {
   186→			continue
   187→		}
   188→
   189→		var req jsonrpcRequest
   190→		if err := json.Unmarshal(line, &req); err != nil {
   191→			continue
   192→		}
   193→
   194→		resp := s.handle(req)
   195→		if resp != nil {
   196→			data, _ := json.Marshal(resp)
   197→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stdout, "%s\n", data)
   198→		}
   199→	}
   200→}
   201→
   202→func (s *server) handle(req jsonrpcRequest) *jsonrpcResponse {
   203→	switch req.Method {
   204→	case "initialize":
   205→		return &jsonrpcResponse{
   206→			JSONRPC: "2.0",
   207→			ID:      req.ID,
   208→			Result: map[string]any{
   209→				"protocolVersion": "2024-11-05",
   210→				"capabilities": map[string]any{
   211→					"tools": map[string]any{},
   212→				},
   213→				"serverInfo": map[string]any{
   214→					"name":    "aileron",
   215→					"version": version.Version,
   216→				},
   217→			},
   218→		}
   219→
   220→	case "notifications/initialized":
   221→		return nil // no response for notifications
   222→
   223→	case "tools/list":
   224→		return &jsonrpcResponse{
   225→			JSONRPC: "2.0",
   226→			ID:      req.ID,
   227→			Result: map[string]any{
   228→				"tools": s.gw.aggregatedTools(),
   229→			},
   230→		}
   231→
   232→	case "tools/call":
   233→		var params callToolParams
   234→		if err := json.Unmarshal(req.Params, &params); err != nil {
   235→			return errorResponse(req.ID, -32602, "invalid params: "+err.Error())
   236→		}
   237→		ctx := context.Background()
   238→		result := s.gw.routeToolCall(ctx, params.Name, params.Arguments)
   239→		return &jsonrpcResponse{
   240→			JSONRPC: "2.0",
   241→			ID:      req.ID,
   242→			Result:  result,
   243→		}
   244→
   245→	case "ping":
   246→		return &jsonrpcResponse{
   247→			JSONRPC: "2.0",
   248→			ID:      req.ID,
   249→			Result:  map[string]any{},
   250→		}
   251→
   252→	default:
   253→		return errorResponse(req.ID, -32601, "method not found: "+req.Method)
   254→	}
   255→}
   256→
   257→// --- Helpers ---
   258→
   259→func argStr(args map[string]any, key string) string {
   260→	v, ok := args[key]
   261→	if !ok {
   262→		return ""
   263→	}
   264→	s, ok := v.(string)
   265→	if !ok {
   266→		return ""
   267→	}
   268→	return s
   269→}
   270→
   271→func jsonResult(v any) toolResult {
   272→	data, _ := json.MarshalIndent(v, "", "  ")
   273→	return toolResult{
   274→		Content: []toolContent{{Type: "text", Text: string(data)}},
   275→	}
   276→}
   277→
   278→func errorResult(msg string) toolResult {
   279→	return toolResult{
   280→		Content: []toolContent{{Type: "text", Text: msg}},
   281→		IsError: true,
   282→	}
   283→}
   284→
   285→func errorResponse(id json.RawMessage, code int, msg string) *jsonrpcResponse {
   286→	return &jsonrpcResponse{
   287→		JSONRPC: "2.0",
   288→		ID:      id,
   289→		Error:   &jsonrpcError{Code: code, Message: msg},
   290→	}
   291→}
   292→

1→// Package app wires together the Aileron control plane components and exposes
     2→// them as an http.Handler. It is imported by the standalone server binary and
     3→// the MCP server's embedded mode.
     4→package app
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"context"
     8→	"log/slog"
     9→	"net/http"
    10→	"os"
    11→
    12→	api "github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/api/gen"
    13→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/approval"
    14→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/connector"
    15→	googlecalendar "github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/connector/calendar/google"
    16→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/connector/git/github"
    17→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/connector/payments/stripe"
    18→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/notify"
    19→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/policy"
    20→	mcpreg "github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/registry"
    21→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/store/mem"
    22→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/vault"
    23→	"github.com/google/uuid"
    24→)
    25→
    26→// NewHandler creates a fully-wired Aileron control plane HTTP handler with
    27→// in-memory stores, seeded policies, and registered connectors.
    28→func NewHandler(log *slog.Logger) (http.Handler, error) {
    29→	ctx := context.Background()
    30→
    31→	// --- In-memory stores ---
    32→	intentStore := mem.NewIntentStore()
    33→	approvalStore := mem.NewApprovalStore()
    34→	policyStore := mem.NewPolicyStore()
    35→	grantStore := mem.NewGrantStore()
    36→	executionStore := mem.NewExecutionStore()
    37→	connectorStore := mem.NewConnectorStore()
    38→	mcpServerStore := mem.NewMCPServerStore()
    39→	credentialStore := mem.NewCredentialStore()
    40→	fundingSourceStore := mem.NewFundingSourceStore()
    41→	traceStore := mem.NewTraceStore()
    42→
    43→	// --- Connector registry ---
    44→	registry := connector.NewRegistry()
    45→	registry.Register(ctx, stripe.New())
    46→	registry.Register(ctx, googlecalendar.New())
    47→	registry.Register(ctx, github.New())
    48→
    49→	// --- Vault ---
    50→	v := vault.NewMemVault()
    51→	if token := os.Getenv("GITHUB_TOKEN"); token != "" {
    52→		v.Put(ctx, "connectors/github/default", []byte(token), vault.Metadata{
    53→			Type: "api_key",
    54→			Labels: map[string]string{
    55→				"connector": "github",
    56→			},
    57→		})
    58→		log.Info("seeded GitHub token into vault")
    59→	}
    60→
    61→	// --- Policy engine ---
    62→	policyEngine := policy.NewRuleEngine(policyStore)
    63→
    64→	if err := policy.SeedPolicies(ctx, policyStore); err != nil {
    65→		return nil, err
    66→	}
    67→	log.Info("seeded default policies")
    68→
    69→	// --- Approval orchestrator ---
    70→	idGen := func() string { return uuid.New().String() }
    71→	orchestrator := approval.NewInMemoryOrchestrator(approvalStore, idGen)
    72→
    73→	// --- MCP Registry client ---
    74→	registryClient := mcpreg.NewClient(nil)
    75→
    76→	// --- Notifier ---
    77→	notifier := notify.NewLogNotifier(log)
    78→
    79→	// --- HTTP handler ---
    80→	mux := http.NewServeMux()
    81→
    82→	server := &apiServer{
    83→		log:            log,
    84→		registry:       registry,
    85→		policyEngine:   policyEngine,
    86→		orchestrator:   orchestrator,
    87→		vault:          v,
    88→		notifier:       notifier,
    89→		intents:        intentStore,
    90→		approvals:      approvalStore,
    91→		policies:       policyStore,
    92→		grants:         grantStore,
    93→		executions:     executionStore,
    94→		connectors:     connectorStore,
    95→		mcpServers:     mcpServerStore,
    96→		registryClient: registryClient,
    97→		credentials:    credentialStore,
    98→		fundingSources: fundingSourceStore,
    99→		traces:         traceStore,
   100→		newID:          idGen,
   101→	}
   102→	api.HandlerFromMux(server, mux)
   103→
   104→	registerDocsRoutes(mux)
   105→
   106→	// Middleware chain: CORS -> request ID -> logging -> routes.
   107→	var handler http.Handler = mux
   108→	handler = loggingMiddleware(log, handler)
   109→	handler = requestIDMiddleware(handler)
   110→	handler = corsMiddleware(handler)
   111→
   112→	return handler, nil
   113→}

1→// Package config loads and validates YAML configuration for the Aileron
     2→// MCP gateway.
     3→//
     4→// A configuration file defines the set of downstream MCP servers that
     5→// Aileron proxies, along with optional policy mappings and environment
     6→// variable overrides. Environment values prefixed with "vault://" are
     7→// resolved from the Aileron vault at launch time via [ResolveVaultEnv].
     8→package config
     9→
    10→import (
    11→	"fmt"
    12→	"os"
    13→	"regexp"
    14→
    15→	"gopkg.in/yaml.v3"
    16→)
    17→
    18→// namePattern matches valid downstream server names: starts with a letter,
    19→// followed by zero or more alphanumeric characters or underscores.
    20→var namePattern = regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_]*$`)
    21→
    22→// vaultPrefix is the sentinel prefix for environment variable values that
    23→// should be resolved from the vault.
    24→const vaultPrefix = "vault://"
    25→
    26→// Execution mode constants for downstream servers.
    27→const (
    28→	// ModeLocal runs the MCP server as a local subprocess (stdio transport).
    29→	// This is the default when Mode is omitted.
    30→	ModeLocal = "local"
    31→
    32→	// ModeRemote connects to a centrally-managed MCP server over HTTP
    33→	// (Streamable HTTP transport). The control plane manages the server
    34→	// instance; no local command is needed.
    35→	ModeRemote = "remote"
    36→)
    37→
    38→// Config is the top-level Aileron gateway configuration.
    39→type Config struct {
    40→	// Version is the config file format version. Currently "1".
    41→	Version string `yaml:"version"`
    42→
    43→	// WorkspaceID is the default workspace for policy evaluation.
    44→	// Defaults to "default" if empty.
    45→	WorkspaceID string `yaml:"workspace_id"`
    46→
    47→	// DownstreamServers lists the MCP servers that Aileron proxies.
    48→	DownstreamServers []DownstreamServer `yaml:"downstream_servers"`
    49→}
    50→
    51→// DownstreamServer defines a single downstream MCP server.
    52→type DownstreamServer struct {
    53→	// Name is a unique identifier for this server, used as tool name prefix.
    54→	// Must be a valid identifier (alphanumeric + underscore, no spaces).
    55→	Name string `yaml:"name"`
    56→
    57→	// Mode is the execution mode: "local" (default) or "remote".
    58→	// Local servers are spawned as subprocesses; remote servers are accessed
    59→	// over HTTP via the control plane. Omitting Mode defaults to "local".
    60→	Mode string `yaml:"mode,omitempty"`
    61→
    62→	// Command is the command to execute, including any arguments.
    63→	// Required for local mode; must be empty for remote mode.
    64→	// Example: ["npx", "-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-github"]
    65→	Command []string `yaml:"command"`
    66→
    67→	// Env is environment variables to set for the subprocess.
    68→	// Values prefixed with "vault://" are resolved from the Aileron vault at launch time.
    69→	Env map[string]string `yaml:"env"`
    70→
    71→	// PolicyMapping configures how this server's tools map to policy evaluation.
    72→	PolicyMapping *PolicyMapping `yaml:"policy_mapping,omitempty"`
    73→}
    74→
    75→// PolicyMapping configures how tools from a downstream server map to the
    76→// policy engine's action type namespace.
    77→type PolicyMapping struct {
    78→	// ToolPrefix maps tool calls to the action type namespace.
    79→	// For example, with tool_prefix "git", a tool "create_pull_request"
    80→	// becomes action type "git.create_pull_request" for policy evaluation.
    81→	ToolPrefix string `yaml:"tool_prefix"`
    82→}
    83→
    84→// Load reads a YAML configuration file from path, unmarshals it, and
    85→// validates the result. It returns the parsed Config or the first error
    86→// encountered.
    87→func Load(path string) (*Config, error) {
    88→	data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
    89→	if err != nil {
    90→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("reading config file: %w", err)
    91→	}
    92→
    93→	var cfg Config
    94→	if err := yaml.Unmarshal(data, &cfg); err != nil {
    95→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("parsing config file: %w", err)
    96→	}
    97→
    98→	if err := cfg.Validate(); err != nil {
    99→		return nil, err
   100→	}
   101→
   102→	return &cfg, nil
   103→}
   104→
   105→// LoadFromEnvOrDefault loads configuration using the following precedence:
   106→//
   107→//  1. The path in the AILERON_CONFIG environment variable.
   108→//  2. "aileron.yaml" in the current working directory.
   109→//  3. An empty Config (no downstream servers) — this is not an error; it
   110→//     allows the gateway to start with no downstream servers configured.
   111→func LoadFromEnvOrDefault() (*Config, error) {
   112→	if path := os.Getenv("AILERON_CONFIG"); path != "" {
   113→		return Load(path)
   114→	}
   115→
   116→	const defaultFile = "aileron.yaml"
   117→	if _, err := os.Stat(defaultFile); err == nil {
   118→		return Load(defaultFile)
   119→	}
   120→
   121→	return &Config{}, nil
   122→}
   123→
   124→// Validate checks the configuration for structural correctness. It returns
   125→// a descriptive error for the first validation failure found.
   126→func (c *Config) Validate() error {
   127→	if c.Version != "" && c.Version != "1" {
   128→		return fmt.Errorf("unsupported config version %q: must be \"1\"", c.Version)
   129→	}
   130→
   131→	seen := make(map[string]bool, len(c.DownstreamServers))
   132→	for i, ds := range c.DownstreamServers {
   133→		if ds.Name == "" {
   134→			return fmt.Errorf("downstream_servers[%d]: name must not be empty", i)
   135→		}
   136→		if !namePattern.MatchString(ds.Name) {
   137→			return fmt.Errorf("downstream_servers[%d]: name %q must match %s", i, ds.Name, namePattern.String())
   138→		}
   139→		if seen[ds.Name] {
   140→			return fmt.Errorf("downstream_servers[%d]: duplicate name %q", i, ds.Name)
   141→		}
   142→		seen[ds.Name] = true
   143→
   144→		// Normalize and validate mode.
   145→		mode := ds.Mode
   146→		if mode == "" {
   147→			mode = ModeLocal
   148→		}
   149→		if mode != ModeLocal && mode != ModeRemote {
   150→			return fmt.Errorf("downstream_servers[%d] (%s): invalid mode %q: must be %q or %q", i, ds.Name, ds.Mode, ModeLocal, ModeRemote)
   151→		}
   152→
   153→		// Local servers require a command; remote servers must not have one.
   154→		if mode == ModeLocal && len(ds.Command) == 0 {
   155→			return fmt.Errorf("downstream_servers[%d] (%s): command must not be empty for local mode", i, ds.Name)
   156→		}
   157→		if mode == ModeRemote && len(ds.Command) > 0 {
   158→			return fmt.Errorf("downstream_servers[%d] (%s): command must be empty for remote mode", i, ds.Name)
   159→		}
   160→	}
   161→
   162→	return nil
   163→}
   164→
   165→// ResolveVaultEnv returns a copy of env with all "vault://"-prefixed values
   166→// resolved through the provided vaultGet function. Non-vault values are
   167→// copied unchanged. The original map is never modified.
   168→func ResolveVaultEnv(env map[string]string, vaultGet func(path string) ([]byte, error)) (map[string]string, error) {
   169→	resolved := make(map[string]string, len(env))
   170→
   171→	for k, v := range env {
   172→		if len(v) > len(vaultPrefix) && v[:len(vaultPrefix)] == vaultPrefix {
   173→			vaultPath := v[len(vaultPrefix):]
   174→			secret, err := vaultGet(vaultPath)
   175→			if err != nil {
   176→				return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolving vault env %q (path %q): %w", k, vaultPath, err)
   177→			}
   178→			resolved[k] = string(secret)
   179→		} else {
   180→			resolved[k] = v
   181→		}
   182→	}
   183→
   184→	return resolved, nil
   185→}
   186→

## Summary

I've found all the key entry points and configuration mechanisms in your codebase. Here's what you need to know:

### 1. **Server Entry Point** - `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/core/server/main.go`

This is the **primary HTTP server**. Key features:
- **Simple startup**: Creates an HTTP server listening on `AILERON_ADDR` (defaults to `:8080`)
- **Graceful shutdown**: Uses signal handlers for SIGINT/SIGTERM
- **Handler creation**: Calls `app.NewHandler(log)` which wires everything together
- **Background goroutine pattern**: Already uses `go func()` to run the server in the background (line 48-53), while the main goroutine waits for signals on `<-ctx.Done()`

### 2. **Application Handler** - `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/core/app/app.go`

This is where **all components are initialized**:
- **Registry client**: Line 74 shows `registryClient := mcpreg.NewClient(nil)`
- Stores, connectors, vault, policy engine, and notifier are all created here
- All components are bundled into `apiServer` struct which implements the HTTP handlers

### 3. **Registry Client Initialization** - `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/core/registry/client.go`

The registry client has:
- **Default settings**: `DefaultRegistryURL = "https://registry.modelcontextprotocol.io"` and `DefaultCacheTTL = 15 * time.Minute`
- **Constructor**: `NewClient(httpClient *http.Client)` creates client with those defaults
- **Cache mechanism**: Uses mutex-protected cache with TTL check before fetching
- **Key methods**: 
  - `FetchAll(ctx)` - fetches all servers, uses cache if fresh
  - `Search(ctx, query)` - filters servers by name/description
  - `Get(ctx, registryID)` - returns single server by name

### 4. **Configuration Mechanism** - `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/core/config/config.go`

The system has **multiple configuration sources**:
- **YAML config file**: Via `Load(path)` - defines downstream MCP servers, modes, commands, environment variables
- **Environment variables**: 
  - `AILERON_ADDR` - server address (from main.go, line 26)
  - `AILERON_CONFIG` - config file path (config.go, line 112)
  - `AILERON_API_URL` - remote Aileron API URL (cmd/aileron-mcp/main.go, line 107)
  - `GITHUB_TOKEN` - vault injection (app.go, line 51)
- **Vault-based secrets**: Config file can reference `vault://path` for secrets resolved at launch time
- **LoadFromEnvOrDefault()**: Tries `AILERON_CONFIG` → `aileron.yaml` → empty config

### 5. **Task Commands** - `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/Taskfile.yml`

Run commands for various operations:
- `task build:server` - builds the server binary locally
- `task up` - starts all services via Docker Compose
- `task test:go` - runs Go tests
- `task health` - curl health endpoint at `http://localhost:8080/v1/health`

---

## How to Add Background Goroutine + Pass Configuration

Based on the codebase patterns:

1. **For a background goroutine at startup**: Follow the pattern in `core/server/main.go` (lines 48-53):
   ```go
   go func() {
       // Your background work here
   }()
   ```

2. **For registry refresh interval**: 
   - The registry client already has a `cacheTTL` field (line 26 in client.go)
   - Currently hardcoded to `DefaultCacheTTL = 15 * time.Minute`
   - You could:
     - Add an **environment variable**: `REGISTRY_CACHE_TTL` and parse it in `app.go`
     - Add a **config file field** to the `Config` struct in `core/config/config.go`
     - Create a constructor like `NewClientWithTTL(httpClient, ttl)` to override the default

3. **To pass configuration through startup**:
   - Add field to `Config` struct in `core/config/config.go`
   - Load from env var in `main.go` before calling `app.NewHandler()`
   - Pass as parameter to `NewHandler()` or embed in a context/config object
   - Inject into `apiServer` and use it when initializing `registryClient`

The cleanest approach would be to:
1. Add `RegistryCacheTTL` to the config file schema
2. Modify `app.NewHandler()` to accept config and pass TTL when creating the client
3. Launch your background goroutine in `NewHandler()` after the registry client is created

  - `AILERON_CONFIG` - config file path (config.go, line 112)
  - `AILERON_API_URL` - remote Aileron API URL (cmd/aileron-mcp/main.go, line 107)
  - `GITHUB_TOKEN` - vault injection (app.go, line 51)
- **Vault-based secrets**: Config file can reference `vault://path` for secrets resolved at launch time
- **LoadFromEnvOrDefault()**: Tries `AILERON_CONFIG` → `aileron.yaml` → empty config

### 5. **Task Commands** - `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/Taskfile.yml`

Run commands for various operations:
- `task build:server` - builds the server binary locally
- `task up` - starts all services via Docker Compose
- `task test:go` - runs Go tests
- `task health` - curl health endpoint at `http://localhost:8080/v1/health`

---

## How to Add Background Goroutine + Pass Configuration

Based on the codebase patterns:

1. **For a background goroutine at startup**: Follow the pattern in `core/server/main.go` (lines 48-53):
   ```go
   go func() {
       // Your background work here
   }()
   ```

2. **For registry refresh interval**: 
   - The registry client already has a `cacheTTL` field (line 26 in client.go)
   - Currently hardcoded to `DefaultCacheTTL = 15 * time.Minute`
   - You could:
     - Add an **environment variable**: `REGISTRY_CACHE_TTL` and parse it in `app.go`
     - Add a **config file field** to the `Config` struct in `core/config/config.go`
     - Create a constructor like `NewClientWithTTL(httpClient, ttl)` to override the default

3. **To pass configuration through startup**:
   - Add field to `Config` struct in `core/config/config.go`
   - Load from env var in `main.go` before calling `app.NewHandler()`
   - Pass as parameter to `NewHandler()` or embed in a context/config object
   - Inject into `apiServer` and use it when initializing `registryClient`

The cleanest approach would be to:
1. Add `RegistryCacheTTL` to the config file schema
2. Modify `app.NewHandler()` to accept config and pass TTL when creating the client
3. Launch your background goroutine in `NewHandler()` after the registry client is created

// Package app wires together the Aileron control plane components and exposes
// them as an http.Handler. It is imported by the standalone server binary and
// the MCP server's embedded mode.
package app

import (
	"context"
	"log/slog"
	"net/http"
	"os"

	api "github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/api/gen"
	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/approval"
	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/connector"
	googlecalendar "github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/connector/calendar/google"
	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/connector/git/github"
	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/connector/payments/stripe"
	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/notify"
	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/policy"
	mcpreg "github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/registry"
	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/store/mem"
	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/vault"
	"github.com/google/uuid"
)

// NewHandler creates a fully-wired Aileron control plane HTTP handler with
// in-memory stores, seeded policies, and registered connectors.
func NewHandler(log *slog.Logger) (http.Handler, error) {
	ctx := context.Background()

	// --- In-memory stores ---
	intentStore := mem.NewIntentStore()
	approvalStore := mem.NewApprovalStore()
	policyStore := mem.NewPolicyStore()
	grantStore := mem.NewGrantStore()
	executionStore := mem.NewExecutionStore()
	connectorStore := mem.NewConnectorStore()
	mcpServerStore := mem.NewMCPServerStore()
	credentialStore := mem.NewCredentialStore()
	fundingSourceStore := mem.NewFundingSourceStore()
	traceStore := mem.NewTraceStore()

	// --- Connector registry ---
	registry := connector.NewRegistry()
	registry.Register(ctx, stripe.New())
	registry.Register(ctx, googlecalendar.New())
	registry.Register(ctx, github.New())

	// --- Vault ---
	v := vault.NewMemVault()
	if token := os.Getenv("GITHUB_TOKEN"); token != "" {
		v.Put(ctx, "connectors/github/default", []byte(token), vault.Metadata{
			Type: "api_key",
			Labels: map[string]string{
				"connector": "github",
			},
		})
		log.Info("seeded GitHub token into vault")
	}

	// --- Policy engine ---
	policyEngine := policy.NewRuleEngine(policyStore)

	if err := policy.SeedPolicies(ctx, policyStore); err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	log.Info("seeded default policies")

	// --- Approval orchestrator ---
	idGen := func() string { return uuid.New().String() }
	orchestrator := approval.NewInMemoryOrchestrator(approvalStore, idGen)

	// --- MCP Registry client ---
	registryClient := mcpreg.NewClient(nil)

	// --- Notifier ---
	notifier := notify.NewLogNotifier(log)

	// --- HTTP handler ---
	mux := http.NewServeMux()

	server := &apiServer{
		log:            log,
		registry:       registry,
		policyEngine:   policyEngine,
		orchestrator:   orchestrator,
		vault:          v,
		notifier:       notifier,
		intents:        intentStore,
		approvals:      approvalStore,
		policies:       policyStore,
		grants:         grantStore,
		executions:     executionStore,
		connectors:     connectorStore,
		mcpServers:     mcpServerStore,
		registryClient: registryClient,
		credentials:    credentialStore,
		fundingSources: fundingSourceStore,
		traces:         traceStore,
		newID:          idGen,
	}
	api.HandlerFromMux(server, mux)

	registerDocsRoutes(mux)

	// Middleware chain: CORS -> request ID -> logging -> routes.
	var handler http.Handler = mux
	handler = loggingMiddleware(log, handler)
	handler = requestIDMiddleware(handler)
	handler = corsMiddleware(handler)

	return handler, nil
}

// in-memory stores, seeded policies, and registered connectors.

	ctx := context.Background()

	intentStore := mem.NewIntentStore()
	approvalStore := mem.NewApprovalStore()
	policyStore := mem.NewPolicyStore()
	grantStore := mem.NewGrantStore()
	executionStore := mem.NewExecutionStore()
	connectorStore := mem.NewConnectorStore()

	registry := connector.NewRegistry()
	registry.Register(ctx, stripe.New())
	registry.Register(ctx, googlecalendar.New())
	registry.Register(ctx, github.New())

	// --- Vault ---
	v := vault.NewMemVault()

				"connector": "github",

		})

	registerDocsRoutes(mux)

	// Middleware chain: CORS -> request ID -> logging -> routes.
	var handler http.Handler = mux
	handler = loggingMiddleware(log, handler)
	handler = requestIDMiddleware(handler)
	handler = corsMiddleware(handler)

file.content:
## Configuration

Aileron is configured with an `aileron.yaml` file that declares downstream MCP servers, credential references, and policy mappings.

```yaml
version: "1"
downstream_servers:
  - name: "github"
    command: ["npx", "-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-github"]
    env:
      GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN: "vault://connectors/github/default"
    policy_mapping:
      tool_prefix: "git"
  - name: "filesystem"
    command: ["npx", "-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem", "/workspace"]
```

Each downstream server entry specifies the command to launch it, environment variables (with optional vault references for secrets), and policy mapping configuration.

## Current Status

The MCP gateway architecture is implemented end-to-end:

- **MCP gateway** aggregates downstream MCP servers and re-exposes their tools
- **Policy engine** evaluates rules against every tool call (allow, deny, require approval, allow with modifications)
- **Approval orchestrator** manages human-in-the-loop workflows with approve/deny/modify
- **Credential vault** injects secrets into downstream servers at launch time
- **Audit store** records every event in an immutable trace
- **Approval UI** provides a web interface for reviewing and acting on pending approvals

Five seed policies ship by default:
1. Require approval for PRs targeting `main`, `master`, or `production`
2. Allow PRs to feature branches
3. Deny force pushes
4. Require approval for tool calls with base argument targeting protected branches
5. Deny destructive tool calls (`delete_*`)

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites

### Environment Variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `AILERON_ADDR` | `:8080` | Address the control plane server listens on |
| `AILERON_CONFIG` | `aileron.yaml` | Path to the configuration file |
| `REGISTRY_REFRESH_INTERVAL` | `15m` | How often the MCP Registry server list is refreshed in the background. Accepts any Go duration string (e.g. `5m`, `1h`). The server prefetches the full registry on startup and refreshes on this interval. |
| `GITHUB_TOKEN` | | GitHub personal access token, seeded into the vault at startup |
